extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won from a late split caused by street furniture crash, capitalizing after VIVIANI Elia lost position in the finale
tactical_patterns: Team Sky and EF Education First preemptively moved to front before street furniture zone, protecting GC riders from crash splits
Quick-Step attempted late leadout for VIVIANI but he became disengaged in final 200m, highlighting positioning fragility in chaotic sprints
TRENTIN exploited VIVIANI's dislocation after crash-induced split gave breakaway survivors better positioning than pure sprint trains

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: veteran awareness to capitalize when primary sprinter loses position; won from non-ideal split situation
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: lost crucial positioning in final despite being in right split group; leadout disconnection cost certain victory
rider_signals.FELLINE Fabio: negative: crashed in street furniture zone, removing SAGAN's key leadout man before finale
rider_signals.LAENGEN Vegard Stake: negative: caught in crash group and burned excessive power to rejoin, compromising stage role for UAE
rider_signals.BENNETT George: positive: safely positioned in front group before crash split, showing improved tactical awareness for GC protection
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: positive: EF working on his behalf suggests strong form; team positioning him defensively before danger zones
